name: Uneven tone & texture
dek: The catch-all category. Pore visibility, surface dullness, fine textural unevenness that the camera reads as 'tired.' Built around exfoliation discipline, retinoid layering, and rejecting the urge to add another acid every fortnight.
oneliner: Vitamin C, AHAs, retinoids. The boring stack works.
hero2: and how to actually fix it.
bottom: Pick one AHA, one retinoid, and one niacinamide. Stick with them for 16 weeks. Stacking five exfoliants does not exfoliate five times harder; it just compromises the barrier.

triggers.k: Pore visibility on T-zone in flat lighting
triggers.n: Often less about pore size than about peri-pore congestion and oxidation. Treat the contents, not the diameter.

triggers.k: Surface roughness on cheeks (felt, not seen)
triggers.n: Stratum corneum disorganisation. Responds to gentle, regular keratolytic exfoliation.
The 'glow' problem. Improvement here usually drives the perceived skincare-is-working signal.
Vascular reactivity that often resolves once the routine simplifies. Sometimes signals barrier compromise.

ingredients.name: Glycolic 8–10% leave-on
ingredients.evidence: Twice-weekly, alternated with retinoid. Reference for tone and texture across 12 weeks.

ingredients.name: Lactic 5–10%
ingredients.evidence: Larger molecule, slower penetration. Better for sensitive skin.

ingredients.name: Mandelic 10%
ingredients.evidence: Best AHA for melanin-rich and reactive skin. Slower endpoints, fewer flares.

ingredients.name: Adapalene / tretinoin
ingredients.evidence: Pore quality and tone respond at 12 weeks. The most under-prescribed step in this category.

ingredients.name: Niacinamide 4–10%
ingredients.evidence: Daily; useful supporting active. Pairs cleanly with retinoid and AHA.

ingredients.name: Microneedling (clinic)
ingredients.evidence: Single best intervention for textural endpoints over 4–6 sessions.

phases.b: Cleanser, niacinamide moisturiser, SPF. Stop physical scrubs and stacked acids.
Adapalene 0.1% PM, alternate nights. The boring foundation under all texture work.
Glycolic 8% leave-on twice a week, on non-retinoid nights. Photograph at week 0 and week 16.
